content.description:
The viewport is the top-level container that frames all user interaction with Hobson. It manages which item is displayed, provides navigation chrome, coordinates context menus, selection, keyboard shortcuts, and the REPL panel.

## Architecture

The viewport is split across several items:

| Item | Role |
|------|------|
| [kernel:viewport-type](item://77777777-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) | Type definition |
| [kernel:system-viewport](item://88888888-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) | Singleton instance — stores current root and REPL state |
| [viewport-view](item://bd74da77-a459-454a-b001-48685d4b536d) | The view — renders chrome, context menus, selection highlighting |
| [viewport-manager](item://f1111111-0002-0000-0000-000000000000) | Library — stateless navigation, URL sync, view config persistence |
| [selection-manager](item://f1111111-0001-0000-0000-000000000000) | Library — tracks selected item and parent |

All of these are userland items, not kernel code. The kernel only knows about the viewport type and instance GUIDs.

## Root Item

The viewport displays one root item at a time. The URL is the authoritative source:

```
hobson.html?root=item-uuid
```

### Resolution Order

1. `?root=` URL parameter (authoritative)
2. `viewport.attachments[0].id` (persisted fallback)

When the URL root differs from the stored root, `viewport-manager` syncs the viewport item to match.

### Navigation

```javascript
api.navigate(itemId);                    // Change root, update URL
api.navigate(itemId, { field: 'code', region: 'myFn' });  // Deep link
```

Navigation updates `history.pushState()`, so browser back/forward buttons work. The `viewport-manager` listens for `popstate` events and re-navigates accordingly.

### Root View Config

The root item's view configuration is stored in `viewport.attachments[0].view`:

```javascript
{
  type: 'spatial-canvas-view-uuid',  // Current view
  innerView: { type: 'note-view-uuid' },  // Background view (for wrapper views)
  // ... other view-specific config
}
```

Accessed via `api.getViewConfig()` / `api.updateViewConfig()` in views.

## Chrome

The `viewport-view` renders minimal chrome around the root item:

- **Browser title** — updates `document.title` to `"{ItemName} - Hobson"`
- **Item decorator** — applies `data-item-id` attributes and tooltips to rendered items for selection targeting and debug inspection
- **Selection highlighting** — `.item-selected` class on the selected item
- **REPL panel** — collapsible panel at the bottom (managed by [repl-ui](item://f1111111-0003-0000-0000-000000000000))
- **Context menus** — see [Context Menus](item://c0c0c0c0-0032-0000-0000-000000000000)

## Selection

The [selection-manager](item://f1111111-0001-0000-0000-000000000000) tracks which item is currently selected:

```javascript
api.viewport.select(itemId, parentId);   // Select an item
api.viewport.getSelection();              // Get selected item ID
api.viewport.clearSelection();            // Clear selection
```

Selection tracks both the item and its parent (important for context-aware operations like "which container is this child in?"). Clicking an item selects it; pressing Escape clears selection.

### Selection Events

The `selection:changed` event fires when selection changes, with payload `{ current: { itemId, parentId }, previous: { itemId, parentId } }`.

## REPL Panel

The REPL panel state persists in `viewport.content.replPanelState`:

| Field | Purpose |
|-------|--------|
| `expanded` | Whether the panel is open |
| `panelHeight` | Panel height in pixels |
| `inputWidthPercent` | Split ratio between input and output |

Toggled via **Esc** key. See [REPL](item://c0c0c0c0-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) for usage.

## Keyboard Shortcuts

The viewport registers global keyboard shortcuts:

| Shortcut | Action | Provided By |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| Cmd+K / Ctrl+K | Open [item palette](item://f1111111-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) | [keyboard-shortcuts](item://f1111111-0004-0000-0000-000000000000) |
| Cmd+? | Open [help dialog](item://f1111111-0006-0000-0000-000000000000) | [keyboard-shortcuts](item://f1111111-0004-0000-0000-000000000000) |
| Esc | Toggle REPL / clear selection / close menu | viewport-view |
| Ctrl+Shift+S | [Safe mode](item://c0c0c0c0-0007-0000-0000-000000000000) escape hatch | Bootloader (hardcoded) |

## Viewport Events

| Event | When | Payload |
|-------|------|---------|
| `viewport:root-changed` | Navigation occurs | `{ rootId, previous, initial, popstate }` |
| `selection:changed` | Selected item changes | `{ current, previous }` |

## Supporting Libraries

```query
const ids = ['f1111111-0001-0000-0000-000000000000', 'f1111111-0002-0000-0000-000000000000', 'f1111111-0003-0000-0000-000000000000', 'f1111111-0004-0000-0000-000000000000', 'f1111111-0005-0000-0000-000000000000', 'f1111111-0006-0000-0000-000000000000', 'f1111111-0007-0000-0000-000000000000'];
const items = [];
for (const id of ids) {
  try { items.push(await api.get(id)); } catch(e) {}
}
return '| Library | Purpose |\n|---------|---------|\n' + items.map(i => {
  const desc = i.content?.description?.split('\n')[0] || 'No description';
  return `| [${i.name}](item://${i.id}) | ${desc} |`;
}).join('\n') + '\n';
```

## Design Rationale

### Why Userland?

The viewport could be kernel code, but keeping it as regular items means:
- The REPL panel, item palette, and help dialog can be modified or replaced
- Context menu operations are inspectable and extensible
- The viewport view itself can be swapped (though this is rare)
- Hot-reload works — editing viewport-view updates behavior immediately

### Why Singleton?

There is exactly one viewport per Hobson instance. It serves as the root of the render tree. Nested instances (via `?instance=`) get their own viewport in their own storage namespace.

### Why URL-Authoritative?

Making the URL the source of truth for the root item enables bookmarking, browser history, sharing links, and multiple entry points — all for free from the browser platform.

## See Also

- [Navigation](item://c0c0c0c0-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) — URL structure, navigation methods, link behavior
- [Context Menus](item://c0c0c0c0-0032-0000-0000-000000000000) — Right-click interaction
- [REPL](item://c0c0c0c0-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) — The interactive console
- [Spatial Windowing](item://c0c0c0c0-0020-0000-0000-000000000000) — The primary container view within the viewport
- [URL Parameters](item://c0c0c0c0-0031-0000-0000-000000000000) — Boot-time parameters including `?root`
